summary: Cantrill announces the first-ever DTrace unconference, dtrace.conf(08), to be held in San Francisco in March 2008. The event is motivated by the growing DTrace ecosystem including ports to new systems, language providers, and new tools. He highlights the unique nature of the DTrace community as systems generalists who think both abstractly and concretely about systems.
key_points: First DTrace unconference announced for March 14, 2008 in San Francisco
DTrace ecosystem expanding with ports, language providers, and new tools
DTrace community characterized as systems generalists with abstract and concrete thinking
money_quotes: Because DTrace operates both across abstraction boundaries and to great depth, it naturally attracts technologists who do the same – systems generalists who couple an ability to think abstractly about the system with a thirst for the concrete data necessary to better understand it.
key_insight: DTrace's ability to work across abstraction layers attracts a unique community of systems generalists who combine abstract thinking with a hunger for concrete data.
